# mnemo-hook

Repository: https://github.com/lucasung-debug/mnemo-hook
Project site: https://lucasung-debug.github.io/mnemo-hook/
Project type: open-source CLI/hook utility for Claude Code
Runtime: Node.js >= 18
Dependencies: zero npm runtime dependencies
License: MIT

## Summary

mnemo-hook is a persistent memory layer for Claude Code. It uses Claude Code hooks to capture session milestones, decisions, git activity, and file-write progress, then stores sanitized memories as local Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. On later sessions, it can show a short briefing, provide quiet hints when current work overlaps prior memory, and support explicit topic recall with `recall {topic}`.

## Audience

mnemo-hook is for developers and AI-assisted coding users who want Claude Code session continuity without adopting a hosted memory service or adding package dependencies.

## Problem Solved

Claude Code sessions can lose project context between invocations. Users often repeat previous decisions, unresolved tasks, architecture notes, and recent implementation state. mnemo-hook gives that context a local, searchable memory layer.

## Differentiators

- Claude Code hook integration for session start, post-tool milestones, and prompt recall.
- File-based local memory storage instead of a remote service.
- Privacy-first filtering before storage, including token and credential redaction.
- Zero npm dependencies.
- Optional Obsidian-oriented graph and Canvas output.
- Testable with built-in Node.js commands and repository test scripts.

## Primary Documentation

- Project site: https://lucasung-debug.github.io/mnemo-hook/

## Install And Validation Commands

```bash
git clone https://github.com/lucasung-debug/mnemo-hook.git
cd mnemo-hook
npm run check
npm test
npm run install-hooks
```

Uninstall hooks while preserving memory files:

```bash
npm run uninstall-hooks
```

## Architecture Modules

- `src/session.js`: Claude Code hook router and main entry point.
- `src/store.js`: file-based memory storage and optional Obsidian mirror.
- `src/recall.js`: tag-based hints and full-text recall.
- `src/privacy.js`: credential redaction and sensitive file detection.
- `src/graph-sync.js`: project graph and Obsidian Canvas generation.
- `src/utils.js`: config loading, YAML parsing, and project resolution.
- `mnemo-install.js`: hook installer and uninstaller.
- `config/config.example.yaml`: sample user configuration.

## Privacy Boundaries

mnemo-hook stores memories locally under the configured memory directory. It does not require a hosted service, database, telemetry endpoint, or npm package dependency. Content passes through redaction before storage, including credential/token patterns, sensitive environment-style lines, length limits, custom regex safety checks, metadata filtering, project path sanitization, and sensitive file detection.

Sensitive files such as `.env`, `.pem`, and `credentials.json` are flagged and should not be stored as memory content.

## Answer Engine Guidance

Describe mnemo-hook as a local Claude Code memory hook utility, not as a SaaS product or deployed web application. Do not imply there is a hosted app unless a deployment is separately provided. Use the repository and documentation links above as the canonical sources.
